## 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

### 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

> **[KTU University Exam Model Question - As per 2024 Scheme Pattern]**

**Q1. [KTU University Exam - Model 2024 Scheme]** *State the two mandatory evaluation touchpoints for Project Based Learning (PBL) under the KTU 2024 Scheme and specify the typical week of the semester in which each is conducted.*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

Under the KTU 2024 Scheme, PBL is evaluated at **two** touchpoints:

1. **Evaluation 1 (PBL-E1):** Conducted **after one month** of commencement of classes — typically in the **4th to 5th week** of the semester. It assesses problem understanding, literature survey, and the plan of work.
2. **Evaluation 2 (PBL-E2):** Conducted **at the end of the course** — typically in the **last week (Week 14–15)** before the University End Semester Examination (ESE). It assesses the final prototype, report, and individual viva.

*(Award 1 mark each for correctly identifying each touchpoint; 1 mark for mentioning the typical week / purpose.)*

---

**Q2. [KTU University Exam - Model 2024 Scheme]** *List any **three** rubric dimensions assessed during the first PBL evaluation (after one month).*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

The three rubric dimensions assessed during **PBL-E1** are:

1. **Problem Definition Clarity** — How well the group has framed the engineering problem.
2. **Literature Survey Depth** — Quality and relevance of background research.
3. **Logbook Maintenance** — Consistency and quality of weekly entries signed by the mentor.

*(Other acceptable dimensions: Plan of Work, Individual Presentation. 1 mark each.)*

---

### 5.2 Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks, with Internal Choice)

> **[KTU University Exam Model Question — Part B Format, 2024 Scheme]**

#### **Question A (14 Marks)** — *PBL Weightage and Marks Computation*

**[KTU University Exam - Model 2024 Scheme - Part B Q4 Module 4]**

**(a)** [7 Marks] *In a 4-member PBL group for the course PBBMT404 (Biosensors & Transducers), the Evaluation 1 raw scores across 5 rubric dimensions are: Problem Definition = 8, Literature Survey = 7, Plan of Work = 9, Logbook = 8, Individual Presentation = 6 (each out of 10). If the total PBL weightage is 10 marks with E1 carrying 40% weightage, calculate the normalized E1 marks for the group.*

**(b)** [7 Marks] *During Evaluation 2, the same group scored a raw total of 48 out of 60. Their group quality factor was G = 0.80, and Student Riya received an individual viva score factor of $I_{Riya} = 0.85$ with a contribution factor of $C_{Riya} = 0.90$. Compute Riya's final PBL marks and state whether she has cleared the PBL pass requirement of 40% of the PBL weightage.*

---

**Model Solution:**

**(a) [7 Marks]**

Given:
- Raw scores: 8 + 7 + 9 + 8 + 6 = **38 out of 50**
- $M_{PBL} = 10$, $w_1 = 0.40$

Normalized group E1 mark:

$$
S_1 = R_1 \cdot \frac{M_{PBL} \cdot w_1}{R_1^{max}}
$$

$$
S_1 = 38 \times \frac{10 \times 0.40}{50}
$$

$$
S_1 = 38 \times \frac{4}{50}
$$

$$
S_1 = 38 \times 0.08
$$

$$
\boxed{S_1 = 3.04 \text{ marks}}
$$

*(Award 7 marks: 2 for summation, 2 for substitution, 2 for final value, 1 for unit/dimensional consistency.)*

---

**(b) [7 Marks]**

Given:
- $R_2 = 48$ out of 60
- $w_2 = 0.60$
- $G = 0.80$, $I_{Riya} = 0.85$, $C_{Riya} = 0.90$

Step 1 — Normalize group E2:

$$
S_2 = 48 \times \frac{10 \times 0.60}{60} = 48 \times 0.10 = 4.80 \text{ marks}
$$

Step 2 — Riya's individual E1:

$$
S_{1,Riya} = S_1 \cdot C_{Riya} = 3.04 \times 0.90 = 2.736 \text{ marks}
$$

Step 3 — Riya's individual E2:

$$
S_{2,Riya} = S_2 \cdot G \cdot I_{Riya} = 4.80 \times 0.80 \times 0.85
$$

$$
S_{2,Riya} = 4.80 \times 0.68 = 3.264 \text{ marks}
$$

Step 4 — Final PBL mark:

$$
M_{final,Riya} = 2.736 + 3.264 = 6.000 \text{ marks}
$$

Step 5 — Pass check:

$$
M_{pass} = 0.40 \times 10 = 4 \text{ marks}
$$

Since $6.000 > 4$, **Riya clears the PBL pass requirement comfortably** ✅

*(Award 7 marks: 2 for E1, 2 for E2, 1 for final summation, 1 for pass-check logic, 1 for verdict.)*

---

#### **Question B (14 Marks)** — *PBL Process and Significance*

**[KTU University Exam - Model 2024 Scheme - Part B Alternative Choice]**

**(a)** [7 Marks] *Explain the pedagogical purpose of conducting **two** PBL evaluations (one after one month and one at the end) rather than a single terminal evaluation. Justify your answer with reference to continuous internal assessment principles.*

**(b)** [7 Marks] *Describe the **six rubric dimensions** assessed during the second PBL evaluation (PBL-E2) in the course PBBMT404. For each dimension, state what a faculty examiner would specifically look for.*

---

**Model Solution:**

**(a) [7 Marks]**

The **bimodal evaluation structure** serves multiple pedagogical purposes:

1. **Continuous Assessment Principle (3 marks):** Education research shows that *distributed practice* (assessing multiple times across a semester) yields better long-term retention than *massed terminal assessment*. Evaluation 1 forces the student to engage from Week 1 itself, preventing last-minute cramming.

2. **Early Remediation Window (2 marks):** If a group scores poorly in E1, the mentor still has ~10 weeks to guide them. A single terminal evaluation would leave no room for course correction.

3. **Process vs. Product Balance (2 marks):** E1 assesses the *process* (how the group is working), while E2 assesses the *product* (what they delivered). A single evaluation would conflate these distinct learning dimensions.

*(Total 7 marks distributed across the three points.)*

---

**(b) [7 Marks]**

The **six rubric dimensions** of PBL-E2 for PBBMT404 are:

| **#** | **Dimension** | **What the Examiner Looks For** |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Prototype / Model Delivery | Functional biosensor hardware or simulation output, electrode fabrication quality |
| 2 | Design Soundness | Circuit design, enzyme immobilization chemistry, transducer selection rationale |
| 3 | Testing & Validation | Calibration curve, sensitivity (mV/mM or µA/mM), selectivity tests, real-sample analysis |
| 4 | Final Report | IEEE/Elsevier format, literature citations, grammar, methodology clarity |
| 5 | Working Demonstration | Live sensor response to analyte, real-time data logging, stability over time |
| 6 | Individual Viva Voce | Each member's *personal* understanding of THEIR OWN work (not the whole group) |

*(Award 1 mark for each dimension name, and ~0.2 marks for the corresponding "look-for" detail. 6 dimensions × 1 mark + 1 mark for concluding synthesis = 7 marks.)*

---

### 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Where KTU 2024 Examiners Typically Deduct Marks in PBL Theory Questions:**
>
> 1. **Confusing the weightages:** Many students write "E1 carries 60% and E2 carries 40%" — this is **WRONG** for most engineering courses in the 2024 scheme. The default is **E1 = 40%** and **E2 = 60%**. Get this wrong and you lose 2 marks instantly.
>
> 2. **Forgetting the individual viva:** When asked about PBL-E2, students often list 5 dimensions and forget the **individual viva voce**. This is a critical 1-mark deduction.
>
> 3. **Ignoring the pass criterion:** Marks computation answers must explicitly end with a **pass/fail statement** comparing $M_{final}$ to $M_{pass} = 0.40 \cdot M_{PBL}$. Examiners *specifically look* for this conclusion.
>
> 4. **Mixing up the CIE total:** Some students write "CIE is 25 marks" (the 2019 scheme value) instead of the **2024 scheme's 50 marks**. This is a date-sensitive error and costs 1–2 marks.
>
> 5. **Not mentioning the logbook:** PBL-E1 *requires* a logbook verification — if your answer omits this, you lose 1 mark.

### 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid-Revision Checklist)

> **🔥 Final 2-Minute Rapid Revision — KTU 2024 PBL Module 4**

- ☐ **PBL = Project Based Learning**, mandatory under KTU 2024 NEP scheme.
- ☐ **Group size:** 4–5 students per project batch.
- ☐ **Evaluation 1 (E1):** After **one month** of class commencement (Week 4–5).
- ☐ **Evaluation 2 (E2):** At the **end of the course** (Week 14–15, before ESE).
- ☐ **E1 weightage:** **40%** of PBL marks.
- ☐ **E2 weightage:** **60%** of PBL marks.
- ☐ **Total CIE aggregate:** **50 marks** (under KTU 2024 scheme).
- ☐ **Typical PBL weightage within CIE:** **10–15 marks**.
- ☐ **Pass requirement in PBL:** **40%** of PBL weightage (e.g., ≥ 4 out of 10).
- ☐ **E1 rubric dimensions (5):** Problem definition, Literature survey, Plan of work, Logbook, Individual presentation.
- ☐ **E2 rubric dimensions (6):** Prototype, Design, Testing, Report, Demonstration, **Individual viva**.
- ☐ **Individual viva is mandatory in E2** — defends personal contribution.
- ☐ **Logbook is mandatory and signed weekly** — checked in both E1 and E2.
- ☐ **Attendance prerequisite:** ≥ **75%** in the parent course.
- ☐ **Marks formula (individual):** $M_{final,i} = w_1 \cdot (R_1 \cdot C_i) + w_2 \cdot (R_2 \cdot G \cdot I_i)$.
- ☐ **PBL marks flow into the CIE 50**, which contributes 50% to the final grade.
- ☐ **PBL is continuous assessment** — bimodal structure ensures process + product evaluation.
- ☐ **Remediation possible after E1** — course correction window of ~10 weeks.
- ☐ **Group quality factor (G)** and **Individual viva factor (I)** prevent free-riding.
- ☐ **PBL Coordinator** at department level consolidates and uploads marks to KTU portal.
